summary: Small decomp-matching PR touching item, menu event, and toy code. It added explicit void-use casts for assigned locals in two functions, reordered declarations in toy.c, and folded a ToyGlobals assignment into an if condition. The bot report recorded three percentage improvements but no full matches.

key_files.role: Adds an explicit `(void) count;` after assigning `count = attr->x14;` inside `it_80289BE8`.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists it_80289BE8 improvement from 99.37% to 99.53%

key_files.role: Adds an explicit `(void) page;` after assigning `page = user_data->page;` inside `mnEvent_8024E524`.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists mnEvent_8024E524 improvement from 99.40% to 99.48%

key_files.role: Reorders local declarations in `un_80310324` and changes `tg2 = un_804D6ED8; if (tg2->x54 == NULL)` into `if ((tg2 = un_804D6ED8)->x54 == NULL)`.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists un_80310324 improvement from 99.29% to 99.63%

what_changed.text: Added `(void) count;` in `it_80289BE8` immediately after loading `attr->x14` into `count`.
Added `(void) page;` in `mnEvent_8024E524` immediately after loading `user_data->page` into `page`.
Moved declarations for `idx`, `tg3`, and `i` earlier in `un_80310324`.
Folded assignment of `tg2 = un_804D6ED8` into the null-check condition on `tg2->x54`.
Automated report recorded three unmatched-item score improvements and no 100% matches.

smart_moves.text: Kept changes very narrow: one small codegen-shape edit per touched function, with no broader type, naming, or data ownership churn visible in the slice.
The PR title accurately warned that these were improvements rather than completed matches.

decomp_lessons.text: For near-matching functions, local declaration order and assignment expression placement can affect score, as shown by the `un_80310324` declaration reorder and assignment-in-condition change.
Explicit `(void)` use of an otherwise assigned local may be a narrow matching tactic, but this PR only supports it as a local codegen experiment for `count` and `page`, not as general style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Use `(void) local;` immediately after an assignment as a possible local lifetime/codegen nudge only when verifier output shows an improvement.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Loaded evidence shows score improvements but not assembly diffs, exact mechanism, or 100% matches.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Try moving local declarations earlier/later in a near-matching function when stack/register allocation appears sensitive.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This is a codegen tactic and should remain evidence-backed, not a style rule.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Try assignment inside a condition when it matches a plausible authored C shape and improves a near match.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR does not prove this was original source style; it only records an improvement.

follow_up_queries: Search for other near-matching functions using `(void) local;` immediately after assignment to determine whether this is a recurring accepted tactic or isolated score steering.
Inspect current `src/melee/ty/toy.c` around `un_80310324` for later cleanup or matching completion after PR 2761.
Compare later PRs touching `it_80289BE8`, `mnEvent_8024E524`, and `un_80310324` to see whether these tactics were retained or replaced.

notes: PR body is empty and there are no review comments.
The bot report provides percentage improvements, but no loaded assembly diff, objdiff detail, or validation log explains why each edit helped.
The PR title explicitly says there were no 100% matches.
No reviewer feedback is available in the loaded slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2761: narrow codegen tweaks improved three unmatched functions without reaching 100%
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2761, `(void)` uses after assigned locals in `it_80289BE8` and `mnEvent_8024E524`, plus declaration-order and assignment-in-condition changes in `un_80310324`, corresponded with bot-reported percentage improvements. Treat as local historical evidence for matching experiments, not as a global style rule.

rejection_notes: Do not promote `(void) local;` after assignment as an accepted global standard from this PR alone; evidence only shows narrow score improvements.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; no reviews or review comments are loaded.
Do not claim these functions matched; the title and bot report indicate improvements short of 100%.
